message:
fix(abi): refine pthread, signal, and unistd ABI implementations

- signal_abi.rs: restructure signal handling logic for improved clarity
  and correctness in signal set manipulation and sigaction dispatch
- pthread_abi.rs: expand pthread ABI with additional thread attribute
  and synchronization operations
- unistd_abi.rs: extend unistd ABI coverage with additional POSIX
  syscall wrappers
- Sync beads issue tracker

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Deliverable: either a fix or a sharply proven failure signature with focused regression coverage.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:43:17.904919513Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:43:17.904919513Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["linux","signal","trampoline"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2gjs.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2gjs.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:43:17.904919513Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2gjs.5.3","title":"R16 closure: prewarm host bootstrap before live signal storm","description":"Move host symbol/bootstrap resolution for the live malloc-storm signal test out of the hostile async-delivery window, or otherwise make the first allocator-path host bootstrap deterministic before signals begin. Deliverable: a concrete bootstrap strategy with regression coverage proving the worker thread no longer enters raw host bootstrap during the first signalled malloc path.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:43:40.396247766Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:43:40.396247766Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bootstrap","host-resolve","signal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2gjs.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2gjs.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:43:40.396247766Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2gjs.5.4","title":"R16 closure: prewarm host bootstrap before live signal storm","description":"Move host symbol/bootstrap resolution for the live malloc-storm signal test out of the hostile async-delivery window, or otherwise make the first allocator-path host bootstrap deterministic before signals begin. Deliverable: a concrete bootstrap strategy with regression coverage proving the worker thread no longer enters raw host bootstrap during the first signalled malloc path.","notes":"Added allocator bootstrap prewarm hook in malloc_abi.rs plus cache-introspection hook for tests. The ignored live-storm test now prewarms allocator delegates and asserts they are non-zero before spawning the worker/signaler threads. Outcome: prewarm is active enough that the ignored test does not fail fast on the cache assertion, but strict/hardened live pthread_kill repro still SIGSEGVs. This means removing the first-pass allocator bootstrap from the hot path was not sufficient to eliminate the crash; remaining investigation moves to real kernel-delivered signal/trampoline behavior.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:44:24.124900261Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:49:23.692040414Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bootstrap","host-resolve","signal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2gjs.5.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2gjs.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:44:24.124900261Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-2gjs.5.5","title":"R16 closure: audit kernel-delivered signal trampoline path","description":"Systematically audit the real kernel-delivered signal path for SIGUSR1 under pthread_kill delivery, including trampoline install semantics, restorer metadata, handler replay, and raw-syscall interactions. Deliverable: either a fix or a sharply proven failure signature with focused regression coverage.","notes":"Added a real kernel-delivered SIGUSR1 smoke test: pthread_kill_delivers_sigusr1_without_allocator_pressure. Result via rch with isolated target dir: FAIL (pthread_kill returns success but installed SIGUSR1 handler never runs; counter stays 0). This proves the remaining blocker is not allocator-specific: real pthread_kill delivery through the current sigaction/trampoline path is broken even without allocator pressure. Also added a first-pass x86_64 Linux kernel-vs-glibc sigaction layout conversion in signal_abi.rs and verified that the existing deferred-delivery smoke still passes (rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_signal_smoke_sunny2 cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test signal_abi_test signal_delivery_is_deferred_inside_critical_section -- --nocapture [PASS]). However the new real-delivery smoke still fails, so further trampoline/kernel-action auditing remains required.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:44:31.489070218Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:56:24.778014154Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["linux","signal","trampoline"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2gjs.5.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2gjs.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:44:31.489070218Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-2gjs.5.5","title":"R16 closure: audit kernel-delivered signal trampoline path","description":"Systematically audit the real kernel-delivered signal path for SIGUSR1 under pthread_kill delivery, including trampoline install semantics, restorer metadata, handler replay, and raw-syscall interactions. Deliverable: either a fix or a sharply proven failure signature with focused regression coverage.","notes":"Added a real kernel-delivered SIGUSR1 smoke test: pthread_kill_delivers_sigusr1_without_allocator_pressure. Result via rch with isolated target dir: FAIL (pthread_kill returns success but installed SIGUSR1 handler never runs; counter stays 0). This proves the remaining blocker is not allocator-specific: real pthread_kill delivery through the current sigaction/trampoline path is broken even without allocator pressure. Also added a first-pass x86_64 Linux kernel-vs-glibc sigaction layout conversion in signal_abi.rs and verified that the existing deferred-delivery smoke still passes (rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_signal_smoke_sunny2 cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test signal_abi_test signal_delivery_is_deferred_inside_critical_section -- --nocapture [PASS]). However the new real-delivery smoke still fails, so further trampoline/kernel-action auditing remains required.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:44:31.489070218Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T14:17:28.829358738Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["linux","signal","trampoline"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2gjs.5.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2gjs.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:44:31.489070218Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1365,"issue_id":"bd-2gjs.5.5","author":"ubuntu","text":"Verified the real kernel-delivered SIGUSR1 path with rch + strace. Root cause was raw x86_64 rt_sigaction installs for non-default handlers leaving SA_RESTORER/restorer unset, so pthread_kill delivery returned into an invalid restorer path and immediately faulted before the user handler counter advanced. Fixed signal_abi.rs to install an explicit x86_64 naked rt_sigreturn restorer trampoline whenever we rewrite a user handler to our signal trampoline and no valid restorer is present. Targeted rch verification now passes for pthread_kill_delivers_sigusr1_without_allocator_pressure plus the immediate/deferred signal delivery smoke tests; cargo check --workspace --all-targets, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings, and cargo fmt --check also pass. Workspace-wide tests are still running; current observed failures are in pthread/process lanes outside this specific fix.","created_at":"2026-04-03T14:08:34Z"},{"id":1366,"issue_id":"bd-2gjs.5.5","author":"ubuntu","text":"Follow-up after the restorer fix: the broader pthread ABI suite still exposed one signal-adjacent regression in sigqueue_live_thread_preserves_queued_value, but it turned out to be a second native-path packing bug rather than a new delivery-path bug. Both pthread_sigqueue and sigqueue were encoding the SI_QUEUE payload as if sigval_t followed si_uid immediately; on 64-bit Linux the queued sigval_t is 8-byte aligned, so there is a 32-bit hole after si_uid and the payload belongs in words 6/7, not 5/6. Patched both ABI entrypoints accordingly. Verification: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_pthread_abi_lavendermarsh2 cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test pthread_abi_test -- --nocapture now keeps sigqueue_live_thread_preserves_queued_value green; remaining failures in that binary are unrelated attr/cancel lanes (cond_init_rejects_destroyed_attr, mutex_init_rejects_destroyed_attr, mutex_init_rejects_unsupported_extension_attributes, cancel_running_thread). Additional gates: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_check_lavendermarsh2 cargo check -p frankenlibc-abi --tests PASSED, rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_workspace_check_lavendermarsh3 cargo check --workspace --all-targets PASSED, rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_workspace_clippy_lavendermarsh3 cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings PASSED, rch exec -- cargo fmt --check PASSED.","created_at":"2026-04-03T14:17:28Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2gjs.5.6","title":"R16 closure: live storm proof and evidence gate","description":"Turn the ignored malloc-storm live pthread_kill repro into a passing acceptance test or replace it with a stronger deterministic strict/hardened proof path that satisfies the bead acceptance criteria for deferred delivery, no deadlock, and no loss. Deliverable: test/e2e commands, structured logs, deterministic artifacts, and closure-ready notes.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:45:06.726124056Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:45:06.726124056Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","evidence","signal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2gjs.5.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2gjs.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:45:06.726124056Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2gjs.6","title":"R17: Terminal/PTY subsystem - rough-path signatures","description":"Apply rough-path signature theory to the terminal and PTY subsystem. Surface: FrankenLibC provides termios manipulation, PTY allocation (openpty/forkpty), and terminal I/O (tcgetattr/tcsetattr, cfsetispeed/cfsetospeed). Failure Class: incorrect terminal mode transitions (cooked/raw/cbreak state machine violations), race conditions in PTY master/slave coordination, and garbled output under high-throughput terminal I/O. Alien Math: Model the terminal I/O stream as a rough path — a mathematical object that captures the 'signature' of a data stream including its sequential and cross-correlational structure. The signature of the terminal mode transitions forms a graded algebra element. The key insight: the signature uniquely characterizes the mode transition history, enabling detection of illegal state machine transitions by checking whether the current signature lies in the 'legal signature set' (pre-computed from the POSIX termios state machine). Runtime Artifact: A compiled signature comparator that evaluates the truncated log-signature of recent mode transitions and checks membership in the legal set. O(1) evaluation per mode change. Detects illegal transitions that would silently corrupt terminal state.

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","design":"**Alien CS Reference:** Graveyard R17. Rough-path theory from Lyons (1998). Path signatures from Chen (1954) / Lyons et al. (2007). Application to stream characterization and anomaly detection.

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- TerminalSignature: truncated log-signature of mode transition history, stored as fixed-size array [f64; SIG_DIM].
- Legal signature set: precomputed from POSIX termios state machine, stored as const BTreeSet<SignatureClass>.
- Update on each tcsetattr/cfsetispeed/cfsetospeed call: extend path with new mode transition vector, recompute truncated signature.
- Anomaly detection: if current signature not in legal set, log warning and optionally block the transition.
- SIG_DIM determined by truncation level (level 2 sufficient for termios: ~10 dimensions).","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
1. Legal signature set captures all valid POSIX termios transitions (raw, cooked, cbreak, echo on/off, etc.).
2. Illegal transitions detected: e.g., setting output baud without input baud in canonical mode.
3. Signature computation <100ns per mode change (infrequent operation, relaxed budget).
4. No false positives for standard terminal setup sequences (bash, vim, screen, tmux init patterns).
5. Signature comparator correctly identifies sequence equivalence (same transitions in different order if order-independent).
6. Test with real terminal programs via PTY: verify no blocking of legitimate mode changes.
7. Documentation of signature algebra in docs/terminal_signature_algebra.md.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"**Logging Requirements:**
- tracing::debug!(target: terminal_signature, fd, transition, new_signature_class, is_legal) on each mode change.
- tracing::warn!(target: terminal_signature, fd, illegal_transition, current_signature, attempted_mode) on illegal transition.

### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:22:00.527069397Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:07:45.545040986Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2gjs.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2gjs","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2gjs.7","title":"R18: SIMD multiarch - Clifford algebra constraints","description":"Apply Clifford algebra constraints to SIMD multiarch optimization. Surface: FrankenLibC's string and memory operations (memcpy, strlen, memset, memcmp) need architecture-specific SIMD implementations (SSE4.2, AVX2, AVX-512, NEON, SVE). Failure Class: incorrect SIMD lane semantics across architectures, misaligned access faults, incorrect predication/masking on variable-width SIMD (SVE), and performance regression when wrong SIMD path is selected at runtime. Alien Math: Model SIMD operations as elements of a Clifford algebra Cl(n,0) where n = number of SIMD lanes. Each SIMD operation (shuffle, blend, permute, compare) corresponds to a Clifford algebra operation (rotation, reflection, projection). The algebra's structure automatically encodes lane-crossing constraints — operations that don't commute in the algebra don't commute in hardware. Use this to: (a) verify SIMD implementations are equivalent across architectures by checking algebraic equivalence, (b) generate optimal SIMD sequences by algebraic simplification. Runtime Artifact: A CPUID-dispatched function table where each entry's correctness is proven equivalent to the reference implementation via Clifford algebra isomorphism certificate.

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","design":"**Alien CS Reference:** Graveyard R18. Clifford algebra Cl(n,0) from Hestenes & Sobczyk (1984). Application to SIMD verification from Franchetti et al. (2005) SPIRAL project. Algebraic equivalence for cross-architecture SIMD.

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- CliffordElement<const N: usize>: represent SIMD operations as elements of Cl(N,0) where N = lane count.
- Isomorphism certificate: build.rs computes algebraic equivalence between x86 and ARM SIMD implementations, stores as const proof.
- CPUID-dispatched function table: FnDispatch<F> selects optimal implementation at runtime based on CPU features.
- Each dispatch entry annotated with its Clifford algebra representation for verification.
- SIMD implementations: use core::arch::{x86_64, aarch64} intrinsics behind cfg(target_arch).","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
1. Clifford algebra model covers SSE4.2, AVX2, and NEON lane operations for memcpy/strlen/memcmp.
2. Algebraic equivalence verified for at least 3 functions across x86-64 and aarch64.
3. CPUID dispatch selects correct implementation (test by masking CPU features).
4. Performance: SIMD memcpy within 10% of glibc optimized version for 1KB-1MB copies.
5. Correctness: all SIMD implementations produce identical results to scalar reference for all input alignments.
6. No misaligned access faults on any input alignment (test with all 16 byte offsets).
7. Isomorphism certificate regenerated when SIMD implementation changes (CI enforces).

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"**Logging Requirements:**
- Build-time: simd_isomorphism_audit.json listing functions, architectures, algebraic equivalence results.
- Runtime: tracing::info!(target: simd_dispatch, function, selected_impl, cpu_features) once at startup.
- No per-call logging (SIMD functions are too hot).

### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:22:10.008463873Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:07:45.319670059Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2gjs.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2gjs","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
